So I played the free version for over 2 hours and was hooked had to stop only due to my head starting to hurt, I looked into it the next night to get the pro version, I never thought I'd be buying a VR game on here, but I want more guns for the free mode it gives you, cant wait to see what else this game has in store.

Could you maybe be able to add in an SMG i think that would be amazing!!

Spatial Zombies Pro is a very good mixed-reality game that delivers genuinely scary and jumpy moments. Playing it on the Meta Quest 3S makes the experience feel intense and immersive, as zombies appear right in your own space, which really keeps you on your toes.

One of the most impressive features is how the game uses your actual doors and windows, making everything feel incredibly real. Having zombies burst through your own environment is seriously unsettling — they even came out of my pantry door in the kitchen, which genuinely made me jump! This level of integration takes immersion to another level and makes every play session feel unique.

The atmosphere is excellent, with strong sound design and sudden surprises that create constant tension. It’s the kind of game that keeps you alert the entire time, never letting you fully relax.

The main downsides are that the selection of weapons could be bigger, as more variety would add depth and replayability. It would also be great to see more variation in the zombies as the game progresses, since encounters can start to feel a bit repetitive after a while.

Overall, it’s a highly enjoyable and thrilling experience that fans of horror and mixed-reality games will definitely appreciate — just be prepared for plenty of jump scares!

The gameplay is fun and exciting, my friends get scared especially when you get poked by zombies from behind. 
The only thing I can add is, the levelling up should be better, harder to kill zombies, increased speed and more zombie types

Great game, such an adrenaline rush, especially when I realize a zombie is right behind me and I get instant goosebumps every time. The guns feel good too, aiming with the laser pointer is very satisfying, especially hitting headshots. And now a sword has been added! Slicing through the zombies is so fun. I get a great workout playing this too. I love it. My only complaint is there needs to be more variety of zombies, it seems like there are three or four over and over and that's it. There needs to be a feeling of leveling up to bigger threats/bosses, like larger zombies, monsters, stuff like that. The graphics are fantastic, I would love to see more backgrounds too. Really great game. I can't imagine anyone not loving this.

Would be awesome to have customization options for the waves like:

1. Zombie Health Modifier
2. Headshots only
3. Zombie Speed Modifier
4. Damage Modifier
5. Health Regent or Manual Health
6. No Lazer
This is an absolute great zombie game out there my number 1 game in my library and simply the best worth the money.

So far I have really enjoyed the game. I would like to see better graphics on the areas outside my room. The fog just doesn't work for me. Looking forward to more upgrades. I would definitely buy again and recommend.

Many zombie games exist, but this game's biggest advantage lies in its extremely fast startup speed and incredibly simple interface and rules. Players can immediately pick up a gun and start shooting zombies without worrying about any complicated settings or operations. I tried the free version and immediately bought the professional version to support the game's development.

I hope there will be more background styles available; currently there are only two. I also hope the VR room design option in the customization settings can be made more realistic.


In short, this is a great game. I look forward to future bug fixes and updates. Thank you to the developers of this game.
The thing that will take this game to the next level is Mixed Reality multiplayer. Co location Zombies would make this the best game in the Meta Store. Take notes from Spatial Ops

It feels like playing real-life COD Zombies in your own house or backyard. You can play in full VR or in mixed reality, where the game overlays onto your actual room. Watching zombies bust through your walls and doors is wild and way more immersive than I expected.

The mixed reality mode is my favorite because I don’t get motion sickness at all, and it feels more natural since you’re moving in your real space. It gets hectic fast and keeps you on edge the whole time.

If you like zombie games and want something that turns your home into the battlefield, this is absolutely worth it.

Thid is one of the most impressive and downright fun mixed reality zombie games on Quest, and it has quickly become a favorite of mine.The way it turns your real home into a chaotic battleground is incredibly immersive, and the intense music and ethereal soundscapes keeps the action thrilling from start to finish. Multiroom ability works amazingly well, and it really shines when you start thinking about how to defend different parts of your own home, turning your hallway, living room, and doorways into a strategic defense plan against the invasion.

The weapon handling is a major highlight, the  assault rifle is sic and the pump shotgun inspires boomstick worthy kill streaks , both feel great to use, especially when you fine tune your hand positions for almost perfect aiming. Many other shooters in theQuest 2 tend to (for me at least) have issues when using rifle size guns where tracking goes all derpy (with ir without virtual stock settings)here everything remains fully functional and more than adequate once the action starts flying for my preference The pistol sits in a league of its own, feeling incredibly natural and responsive, which makes lining up clean headshots especially satisfying. The recently added katana is the ultimate baddass ninja tool turning zeds effortlessly into chop suey 

The between-wave store is an awesome addition, letting you send earned money on weapons  grenades and mele weapons.The mix of ranged weapons, explosives, and melee options adds real depth to how you defend your own fortress of solitude encouraging you to plan choke points and fallback positions as the waves ramp up. Online leaderboards add even more motivation, pushing you to refine your play style and  strategy as you chase higher scores.

It is abundantly clear that the developer genuinely cares about the game, continuously refining systems, adding features, and pushing the experience forward with Passion. the work in progress inventory management system already shows serious promise and could end up being one of the best once fully polished. the game uses your existing saved room setup, meaning practically no extra setup at all even with 2 bedrooms ,a hallway, kitchen and living room/dining room,all set up as different spaces on the quest 2 running surprisingly smooth on the aging headset,jumping in for a quick session is fast, seamless, and. phenomenal

For the price, the value is epic and it honestly feels like the experience is worth more than what is being asked. With planned multiplayer and ongoing development, Spatial Zombies already stands out as a top-tier mixed reality shooter and an incredibly exciting look at what MR can do when your own home becomes the front line of the apocalypse. Try the demo if you want butifyur read this far you should just buy it already
Must play game
For $10 this game is awesome. Zombies sneaking up behind you, increased difficulty as you play, zombies in your home. Its great. Would love more variety with the zombies, but overall, love this game
Jump scare fun!
Let’s just say I played this game thinking I wouldn’t get freaked out but boi after you kill one zombie you turn around and there is just one speeding at you 🤣🤣

This game is really fun, especially with shooting zombies in your living room. Even though there is some repetition with the limited game modes, it is so fun I could play this game forever. But, just in case you don’t  like mixed reality stuff, try the free version before buying.

Updated review:
The mixed reality flashlight now moves with your head if(and only if) equipped in one of the two chest holsters. Backpack holster position would not work for me, and returning the flashlight to the chest holster seemed to only work if the holster below it was empty. Not an issue if you keep it in its default position; added crowbar is helpful, but lacks a feeling of impact. Manual reload mechanic has improved. Two handed weapons could still use handling points for immersion. Zombies AI still could use improvement. Hoping this title continues to improve, it's taken a step in the right direction.
                                                                                                                               
_____________________________________________________
                                                                                                                   
Original 2/5 review:                                                                             
There was not enough ammunition to clear rounds and no melee backup option so progress was quickly halted. Weapon handling of the pistol included a cosmetic action, reloading only requires clip to be inserted. No clip exit animation. Two handed weapons snap hands to predefined positions that break immersion, more handling points would help here. Zombies AI made them wander aimlessly in and out of one entry point, hit reaction animations and attacks have no variety. Mixed reality 'darkness' does not allow vertical movement of the flashlight yet its stuck to your head like a turret. I could not look down to see any crates or ammunition, nor any actual obstacles that I might otherwise be able to circumnavigate. Hoping it improves, right now the interface looks nice but the gameplay needs a lot more attention.

For someone who doesnt really play mixed reality games, or horror, and then putting them together, this game suprised me. It kept me on my toes. Giving the option of day time or night time and now horror gives experiences for everyone. For me, daytime, and even that scares me

On custom mode, 20 rounds in but stil didn’t find any crates or other weapons… is it my map or just no crates on custom? otherwise, this game is fantastic. Got me running around our living room at round 16-20…

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Thanks a lot for the great feedback, I really appreciate it!

It’s very likely that the latest update reset your game settings, which would have set the Reload Style back to the default Auto mode. In this mode, there are no ammo crate drops because the game uses a cooldown / heat-based weapon system instead. This is a simpler, more hassle-free option for players who prefer a more relaxed gameplay style.
If you like a more advanced reloading experience, you can switch the Reload Style to Semi-Auto or Manual. In these modes, ammo crates will drop again. Pistol ammo drops automatically, while rifle and shotgun ammo can be purchased in the shop using the coins you earn from each kill.

I hope this helps! If you have any further questions or feedback, feel free to reach out anytime at ft_studio@outlook.de.

Wishing you a wonderful Christmas time.

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_title: Spacial zombies in your home - scary
reviews.review_description:
I didn't expect a game to be this intense.  The zombies are well made and look terrifying coming towards you.  I mapped my whole upstairs and had them coming from every room.  
Having to run around the house was very entertaining and exciting.  

The one thing I found is that progression is a challenge because of the limited ammo and no melee weapons.  Once you run out of ammo it seems you are done for.  No way to protect or attack.

Overall a fun game if you have the space to play.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks so much for your awesome feedback! I’m really glad you’re enjoying the intensity and running around your house — that’s exactly the vibe I wanted to create.

I totally hear you on the ammo challenge. Good news: with the latest update, I’ve improved the ammo balance and added one-shot headshot kills for the weaker zombies. So now you can take them down faster and save ammo for the big threats!

Melee weapons are on my list for future updates to give you even more ways to survive.

Thanks again for playing and sharing your thoughts — hope these changes make your zombie runs even more fun!

reviews.review_description: this game is totally worth your money i definitely  would recommend buying this game
I won a key to this game and it's one of the best games I've played but it can be better, when you have a lot of items it kind of difficult to grab the item you want (they should really fix that)

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I was looking for....
reviews.review_description:
So this game is early access and not without its areas that need improvement of course, but jesus it's fun, even in its current state!!!! And the reviews saying it's "intense" are bang on the money, had me audibly crapping my pants at one point! All that and I haven't even mapped multiple rooms yet, just been surviving the horde in my tiny living room so far!

This is my first review since I bought the Quest 3 and one of the only reviews I have left over the years at all, but I needed to tell people who are on the fence about this game to just DO IT! Particularly now when it's on sale, you will not regret it! Plus the developer seems to be very responsive and takes constructive criticism really well so far when adding updates!

Overall this is exactly the type of game I thought of when I bought my VR and it is largely responsible for me not having buyers remorse so far (got a few other games but not had a chance to play them properly and the ones I did try were not all that good, until now!!!)


Feedback sorry far......

1. Could do with a little more variety on weapons, but not a huge problem at this stage. Developer has mentioned that melee may come soon which will be a huge improvement for realism/immersion.

2. A few more tutorial/hint messages at the beginning, explaining to people new to VR what will happen in mixed reality and how to play, again though not a major thing, will just effect the people with limited gaming experience.

3. Developer should offer a 15 minute free trial 100%. I was on the fence about this game and ended up buying out of the remainder of my quest voucher that came with my headset, on a whim if you will.

But if I had got a 15 minute trial first to see exactly what this game was all about, I would have paid twice what I did IMMEDIATELY!!!!!!

Maybe stats say otherwise about free trials, but for me this would be the best move at this point, people need to see this game ASAP!

Keep up the good work Devs, loving this game!

reviews.review_title: 100% what a zombie shooter should be!
reviews.review_description: I've only been playing this game for half an hour. It's already a blast, me and my son are absolutely having a blast with it . can't wait to try out the multiple room function. Great job guys!
Awesome and fun at the same time

First off, this type of gameplay shines in mixed reality. Fun, makes you move around and get a tiny bit of exercise.

There are some annoyances though that can wreck runs and i'm not sure if these are bugs or if i'm just doing it wrong.

Dropping a weapon sometimes makes it just disappear.

Example: holding the normal gun in right hand, picking up the shotgun with left hand then dropping the shotgun will sometimes just remove the shotgun from the game area.

Sometimes a new weapon box will not spawn at all, could be my map layout or i'm just blind though.

Weapons getting stuck in zombies faces: while more realistic than having them clip through, creates annoying scenarios with longer weapons such as the rifle when you are swarmed with zombies, but this might be intended design.

When running in horror mode its practically impossible to see any crates and the flashlight is positioned in such a way that it only shines forward, so i have ended up not even finishing round 1 sometimes due to not actually finding a weapon.

Overall this MR game got a lot of potential if the creator gives it enough love and care!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the thoughtful feedback — it was super helpful and directly shaped the latest update!

You mentioned the disappearing weapon issue — great catch. What’s actually happening is part of the auto-holster system: when you drop a weapon and move away, it returns to your holster so you don’t lose it. But if that slot is already blocked (like by another weapon or object), the game tries to send it to a free backpack slot instead. Totally understandable that this felt like a bug — I’ve now made the system much clearer with visual cues and a more intuitive layout.

Here’s what’s new based on your feedback:

- Flashlight now follows your head movement for better visibility in horror mode.
- Weapons no longer collide in zombie combat — this makes close-range fights smoother, especially with longer guns like the rifle.
- Holster & backpack systems have been redesigned: better grab range, clearer visual feedback, and smoother item storage — especially when slots are full.
- Crates emit a purple flash and beam into the sky to make them easier to find, especially in dark maps.
- Unused items now flash purple so you can find gear more easily.
- Tutorial images now display during loading screens to help new players pick up the mechanics faster.

Thanks again — your feedback genuinely helps make the game better. I really appreciate the time you took, and I hope the update makes the experience even more fun! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
This game is honestly such a cool idea and it materialized a thought ive had for a while, a walking dead-like experience in your own home. Its pretty cool how such games are remaking the game and how AR and VR can work.

Honestly this game is really cool and it currently has a couple different modes to try out which provide experiences that range from scary to slightly leas scary and i highly suggest everyone try it, however, i do have a couple improvements i want to suggest because i see true potential in this game and i want to help partake in its plan. I enjoyed the gameplay but i found a couple features that sort of broke the immersiveness like how small the gun is and how it somehow grows bigger when you unholster it. I also would love if you could make it so that the zombie wave eases in, because i find it gets rather quite chaotic quick. I would LOVE if you could add melee mechanics because i find that i run out of ammo quick and sometimes i just wanna whack a zombie in the face ya know.

I will also update the developer more in private messages if i see any other rooms for improvements but guys please be more mindful with your reviews, this game is in early access and in its most vulnerable state. This is a solo developer pouring his heart out into this game project so respect that and be mindful of that fact, we should be encouraging indie developers to keep at it rather than put them down, that being said its been a pleasure to play this game and i reckon ill be playing this game as a default now.

(Btw if your home is big enough, the multi room feature is a must-try, its levels ahead in terms of immersion and really gives you a scare)

As of the last update, the game has become really good. The day/night time along with the multiple rooms setup is really fun. The new zombies keep you on your feet and the mechanics have really improved. I really like the style of the doors and windows too amd for anyone wondering you can turn off the guardian lines off in the settings, it took me a sec to think of that

reviews.review_title: Super fun and has so much potential
reviews.review_description: Bear in mind that it's still in early access. For what it is so far, it's so fun and I can't wait for what the dev team will add in the future. It's pretty exciting to see how zombies come out of my bathroom and balcony. It's a great way to just time-out of reality while still kind of being in reality. Keep up the great work 💪🏽🧿
The game has several glitches. You can’t hit any zombies. The “training” room just stops working randomly. Don’t waste your money and your time on this.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your feedback — I really appreciate you taking the time to share it!

I'm sorry to hear you ran into glitches. That’s definitely not how the game is meant to play. If you’re willing, I’d love to hear more details, especially about what happened in the training room.

As a solo developer, I’m always working to improve the game, and your input really helps. Feel free to reach out anytime at FT_Studio@outlook.de — I’d be grateful for the chance to fix things and make the experience better.

Thanks again
Tom

reviews.review_description:
Spatial Zombies offers a unique experience where every step becomes a strategic decision, and every corner of your home could hide a danger, primarily in Mixed Reality (MR) but also in VR. The gameplay focuses on fast-paced shooter action, as you defend yourself against waves of zombies using realistic firearms (with manual or automatic reloading), while managing multiple rooms and utilizing your real-world environment as a battleground.  Several game modes are available, including a fully customizable mode (day or night, VR or MR, music, urban or forest environments, etc.). The Horror mode ramps up the tension with innovative flashlight lighting, either attached to your chest or held in hand. Supply crates appear randomly, providing new weapons, health items, and ammunition. 
Overall, it's an excellent stress-reliever, and the multi-room feature is a truly innovative and impressive concept. 
Seeing zombies roaming around your living room is deliciously terrifying! 
Regular updates add new content and enhance replayability.
My video review: https://youtu.be/O3tHE4K1Wt0

I sometimes don’t say anything about the negative but why do this game have a low rating when this is next level pro mix reality u ask me this is like seriously a great vr game for everyone so stop 🛑 and ￼ purchased this game. You won’t be mad and don’t believe the negative until you try it yourself. Thank guys for a great vr experience

Agree with all the positive reviews!

It is a work in progress and can’t wait for the final product!

More sound effects. From the zombies would be nice!


I’ll reserve the final two stars for the finished product!

It would not scan or calibrate my house.

￼

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks for letting me know you’re having trouble scanning your house. There could be a few reasons for this, so here are some quick things to check:

1. Permissions: Make sure the game has permission to access your space setup on your Meta Quest. This usually pops up the first time you launch the game, but you can also check anytime in Settings > Privacy and Safety > App Permissions > Spatial Data > Spatial Zombies.

2. Space Setup: Double-check that your physical space is properly set up by going to Settings > Environment Setup > Space Setup on your device.

3. Clear Physical Space History: If your space is set up but you’re seeing weird “ghost” walls, rooms, or doors that shouldn’t be there, try clearing your space history. Just head to Settings > Privacy and Safety > Device Permissions > Clear Physical Space History and then set up your room again.

If you still run into issues, don’t hesitate to email me anytime at "FT_Studio@outlook.de" — I’m here to help!

Also, if you manage to get it working, I’d really appreciate it if you could update your review. Thanks so much for playing and supporting the game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the great feedback! I’m glad you’re enjoying the game overall. 
I totally get the frustration with the boundaries – but the good news is, you can now play without boundaries or adjust the sensitivity to your liking! You can easily set this up in the settings menu. 
Hopefully, this will make it much easier to find those crates and give you a smoother experience. Enjoy and have fun! 😊

reviews.review_description: Where do you get more ammo?
During training mode everything works fine but when I try to play a real game I can't move around. What am I doing wrong??

developer_response.body:
Hey! In training mode, you use the controller to move around in virtual reality, but when you start the real game, it’s all about mixed reality. That means you need to physically move around in your room to control your character. 
Just make sure you have enough space and that your device is tracking you properly. If you're still having trouble, feel free to let us know, and we'll help you out! 
Mail: ft_studio@outlook.de

If this solves your issue, we’d really appreciate it if you could update your rating. Those 1-star reviews really hurt, and we’re constantly working to improve the game for everyone! 
Thanks a ton! 😊

reviews.review_description: Used Meta settings for room. Already mapped out the room, but no zombies come. Waited for 5 minutes. Nothing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for letting me know about the issue! I’ve taken care of it in the latest update, so the zombies should now start showing up after you map out the room. Just make sure your game is up to date, and you should be all set. If you run into any more issues, I’m here to help. Have fun, and happy zombie hunting!
